methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Simera Sense is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $16.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of three same-stage, same-sector private peers (Aalyria, Hubble Network, and Sift) and public benchmarks from six space and aerospace equities (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL, and one additional ticker).

Two quantitative methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a $31M valuation. Peer-median valuation across the three private comparables (range: $274M–$1.3B) yields a $400M implied value. Public-comp anchoring could not be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, a common constraint for private companies. The median public-sector P/S multiple in this space stands at 53.2x.

The triangulated analysis across two applicable methods supports a broad range of $26M to $460M, with a median of $215M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: the stage-progression method anchors conservatively to historical capital, while peer-median is heavily influenced by Aalyria's $1.3B valuation. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and whether Simera's technology differentiation warrants positioning toward the upper or lower quartile of peer valuations.
